info.title: Create generic Spoon rule for SnakeYAML 2.0 API migration

parts.text: "Project @simplelocalize-cli/ does not compile due to a breaking dependency update. Your goal is to generate a GENERIC, REUSABLE transformation rule - not a one-off patch - that can be applied to ANY Maven project affected by the same breaking change. Plan: 1) Identify the compilation errors in @simplelocalize-cli/ and determine which API    (class/method/constructor/signature) changed in the dependency.    The new dependency API specification is available at /workspace/snakeyaml-2.0-api.md and the Javadoc at /workspace/javadoc-snakeyaml-2.0/    for reference when identifying replacement types, methods, or fields. 2) Characterize the breaking change abstractly:    - What was the old API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String))    - What is the new API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String, boolean))    - What structural transformation does this require at the call site?    Do NOT assume the fix is specific to this project - any project calling the old API will need the same transformation. 3) Generate a GENERIC Spoon transformation using the template in @/workspace/spoon-base-template/:    - Match the old API pattern structurally, not by project-specific class names.    - Use patterns to traverse all files and apply the fix wherever the old pattern appears.    - Parameterize by fully-qualified type names and method signatures from the dependency, NOT from the client.    - Save the transformation in /workspace/spoon-base-template/src/main/java/github/chains/Main.java.    - Use the Spoon API documentation located in folder /workspace/spoon-api-docs/ for reference. 4) Compile the transformation and verify it has no errors. 5) Apply the transformation to @simplelocalize-cli/ and ensure both compilation errors and test errors are fixed. 6) Ensure generalizability: ensure the generated rule contains no hardcoded project-specific identifiers.    The rule must be applicable to other projects by simply changing the input source directory path."

**Inspecting the repo**

I need to inspect the repository, and I think it’s best to use a to-do list since it’s quite complex. I'll need to pay attention to any commentary updates, too. Maybe I should also explore some options? However, we've got tools like read, glob, and grep available. First, I’ll focus on locating the project and then start with updates and the to-do tasks. Let's get started!
I’m locating the affected project and the Spoon template, then I’ll trace the SnakeYAML 2.0 API break and implement a generic source-to-source rule.
